title: Roasted Cauliflower Mash
description: Creamy, buttery mashed cauliflower that rivals traditional mashed potatoes. A perfect low-carb side dish.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Toss cauliflower florets with 2 tbsp melted ghee and spread on a baking sheet.
Wrap garlic cloves in foil with a drizzle of oil. Place on the baking sheet with cauliflower.
Roast for 25-30 minutes until cauliflower is golden and very tender.
Transfer roasted cauliflower and garlic to a food processor or high-powered blender.
Add remaining ghee, coconut cream, salt, and white pepper. Process until completely smooth, scraping down sides as needed.
Taste and adjust seasonings. If using nutritional yeast for a cheesy flavor, add now and pulse to combine.
Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with chives. Serve warm.

title: Roasted Brussels Sprouts with Olive Oil & Garlic
description: Crispy, caramelized Brussels sprouts roasted with garlic and finished with a squeeze of lemon. Simple perfection.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Toss Brussels sprouts with olive oil, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Spread in a single layer on the baking sheet, cut side down.
Roast for 20 minutes until bottoms are deeply caramelized.
Add minced garlic, toss everything together, and roast for 5 more minutes.
Remove from oven and immediately toss with lemon juice and zest.
Sprinkle with red pepper flakes if desired. Serve hot.

title: Crispy Sweet Potato Fries
description: Perfectly crispy baked sweet potato fries with a hint of spice. A healthier alternative to regular fries.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Line two baking sheets with parchment paper.
Soak sweet potato sticks in cold water for 30 minutes to remove excess starch. Drain and pat completely dry.
Toss dried sweet potatoes with avocado oil and arrowroot powder until evenly coated.
Add paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Toss to coat evenly.
Spread in a single layer on baking sheets, ensuring fries don't touch each other.
Bake for 15 minutes, flip each fry, then bake for another 10-15 minutes until crispy and golden.
Season with additional salt if needed and garnish with parsley. Serve immediately.

title: Garlic Sautéed Greens
description: Quick and nutritious sautéed greens with plenty of garlic. Use any combination of kale, chard, or collards.

ingredients.name: mixed greens
ingredients.notes: kale, chard, collards - stems removed, roughly chopped

instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add sliced garlic and red pepper flakes. Cook for 1-2 minutes until garlic is golden and fragrant (don't let it burn).
Add greens in batches, tossing with tongs as they wilt to make room for more.
Once all greens are added, pour in chicken broth and season with salt.
Cover and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until greens are tender but still vibrant.
Remove lid and cook for another 1-2 minutes to evaporate excess liquid.
Finish with a squeeze of lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasonings. Serve warm.
